hypothesis:
HYPOTHESIS: The integration of compression mechanisms from biology into machine learning models, such as Vision Transformer-based sparse multi-view 3D object detectors, can significantly improve their inference latency and accuracy.

PREDICTION: We expect to find that the application of biological compression mechanisms can reduce the inference latency of ViT-based sparse multi-view 3D object detectors by at least 30% and improve their accuracy by 15% on standard benchmarks.

EXPERIMENT: To test this hypothesis, we will design and implement a novel compression algorithm inspired by the Carlo model and the black-winged kite algorithm, and integrate it into a ViT-based sparse multi-view 3D object detector, which will then be evaluated on the ScanNet and Waymo datasets. The performance of the proposed model will be compared to state-of-the-art models using metrics such as mean Average Precision (mAP) and frames per second (FPS).

IMPACT: This research has the potential to significantly improve the efficiency and accuracy of computer vision models, enabling their deployment in real-time applications such as autonomous vehicles, robotics, and surveillance systems, which can have a major impact on industries such as transportation, healthcare, and security.
